pattern: Every letter shift is just a residue mod 26, and if several letters need the same residue they can't all move on the same turn -- the c-th letter needing residue d can't finish before turn d + 26*(c-1), so the whole question collapses to one counting pass per residue.
hints: Forget about which index moves on which turn -- first work out, for each letter, how many single-step shifts (mod 26) it needs to become the target letter.
Group the letters by that shift amount. Two letters that both need a shift of 5 can never both move on turn 5 -- the second one has to wait until turn 5 + 26.
For a shift amount d shared by c letters, the latest one needs turn d + 26*(c-1). Compute that for every shift amount that actually occurs and compare the worst one against k.

edge_inputs.description: single letter needs one shift but k is zero
two letters need shift 1 -- the second must wait until turn 27, so k=26 must fail and k=27 must pass
same as above but with exactly enough moves
wrap-around from z to a is a shift of 1, not 25
three letters all needing shift 1 -- worst offender needs turn 1+26*2=53

stress.description: random same-length lowercase strings with a random move budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    s = ''.join(chr(ord('a') + rng.randint(0, 25)) for _ in range(n))
    t = ''.join(chr(ord('a') + rng.randint(0, 25)) for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(0, 30 * n)
    return [s, t, k]
